channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula cita integração regional com a Bolívia e diz que nenhum país da América do Sul tem condições de prosperar isoladamente
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva met with Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz at the Palácio do Planalto, emphasizing regional integration as essential for South America's prosperity. Lula stated that no country in the continent can thrive in isolation and stressed cooperation on infrastructure, energy, tourism, and combating organized crime in the Amazon. He also canceled his attendance at the inauguration of Chilean President José Antônio Kast, sending Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira instead.

item.title: Escolhido por Flávio para o governo do RJ, Douglas Ruas privilegia redutos eleitorais à frente de secretaria
item.description: Rio de Janeiro State Cities Secretary Douglas Ruas, chosen by Flávio Bolsonaro to represent the PL in the gubernatorial race, allocated 1.06 billion of the 2.3 billion reais invested by his department to São Gonçalo — where his father, Captain Nelson, is mayor — nearly 50% of the total. Itaboraí received 537.85 million and Belford Roxo 129 million, with the ten largest projects concentrated almost exclusively in these two municipalities, reflecting a strong allocation of resources to the Bolsonaro family's electoral strongholds.

item.title: Há chance para a terceira via?
item.description: The author argues that while a third-way candidacy is not physically impossible, its chances of breaking the polarization between the governing camp and Bolsonarism in 2026 are slim. He avoids declaring failure certain but expresses skepticism about the viability of a candidate outside the two dominant poles.

item.title: Wagner Moura, torcida brasileira e empate; veja momentos do Oscar 2026
item.description: 'Uma Batalha Após a Outra', directed by Paul Thomas Anderson, won six awards at the 2026 Oscars, including Best Picture and Director, portraying a multigenerational saga of political resistance against an authoritarian regime. 'Pecadores', featuring a Black vampire cast in the segregation-era U.S., won four awards, including Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan and Best Original Screenplay for Ryan Coogler. The text also notes a subscriber benefit from Folha de S.Paulo, allowing subscribers to share seven daily accesses with non-subscribers.

item.title: Apesar de resistências do agro e do setor de energia, governo conclui Plano Clima com metas para reduzir emissões
item.description: The federal government has completed the National Climate Plan, which sets targets for reducing greenhouse gas emissions and includes actions to address climate injustice, focusing on vulnerable populations and increasing women's participation. Finalized by the Ministry of Environment under Marina Silva’s leadership, the plan integrates mitigation, adaptation, financing, and sectoral policies aligned with Brazil’s international commitments, including the NDC submitted at COP29.

item.title: Pesquisa revela a força de Flávio Bolsonaro em estados onde o pai venceu
item.description: A Real Time Big Data poll in Espírito Santo shows Flávio Bolsonaro with 42% voting intention versus Lula’s 33% in a presidential scenario, underscoring his inheritance of his father’s political capital in Bolsonaro strongholds. In an alternative scenario, the numbers remain stable at 40% to 33%, indicating Flávio’s consolidation as a competitive challenger in 2026. Analysts note the results reflect Jair Bolsonaro’s electoral legacy in the state, where he won decisively in 2022.

item.title: Com derrota de Wagner Moura, Oscar prova que não ouve o voto popular
item.description: The 2026 Oscar awarded Michael B. Jordan Best Actor, overlooking Wagner Moura’s strong social media popularity, which garnered the highest engagement among nominees. The result reflects a historical pattern in which the Academy’s roughly 10,000 voters—mostly American industry professionals—favor U.S. artists, despite Brazilian candidates like Moura and Fernanda Torres generating greater online buzz. The article criticizes the disconnect between popular sentiment on social media and the Academy’s decisions, arguing the Oscars do not reflect global public taste.

item.title: Senador Paulo Paim vê João Campos como alternativa para suceder Lula em 2030
item.description: Senator Paulo Paim (PT-RS), 75, announced he will not run in the 2026 elections after nearly 40 years in Congress. He advocates for passing the torch to a new generation but criticizes the PT for failing to adequately develop new political cadres. Although he does not endorse João Campos for 2030, he mentions the Recife mayor as a potential future alternative to succeed Lula.

item.title: Cogitado pelo PL em MG admite conversas com Flávio Bolsonaro, mas diz não forçar candidatura
item.description: Flávio Roscoe, president of Fiemg and mentioned as a possible PL candidate in Minas Gerais in a leaked document from Flávio Bolsonaro, admits having spoken with the senator but denies being pressured to run for office. He says he will leave Fiemg next month, before the end of his term, and is evaluating the possibility of running for state governor in 2026. Roscoe stresses that there is no decision or imposition regarding his candidacy.

item.title: Zé Dirceu confirma que será candidato a deputado por São Paulo
item.description: José Dirceu, former Chief of Staff to Lula, confirmed he will run for federal deputy from São Paulo in the 2026 elections, after having his eligibility restored by the STF in 2024 due to the annulment of Lava-Jato cases. He turns 80 this Monday and aims to return to Congress after 22 years of disqualification over his involvement in the mensalão corruption scheme. Other figures implicated in scandals, such as Eduardo Cunha, José Roberto Arruda, and Agnelo Queiroz, have also announced their candidacies for 2026.

item.title: Portal de Dados Abertos do TSE amplia acesso a informações eleitorais e fortalece transparência
item.description: The TSE Open Data Portal provides public access to hundreds of datasets on Brazilian elections, including candidacies, results, party finances, and electoral research, in open formats. The initiative enhances transparency, combats misinformation, and supports academic research, journalism, and civic engagement through intuitive search and navigation tools.

item.title: Pré-candidato ao governo de MG tem camisa rasgada e leva mordida durante briga; assista
item.description: Ben Mendes, pre-candidate for governor of Minas Gerais from the Missão party, was physically assaulted by a woman in a store in Betim following a dispute over merchandise pickup. In the video he shared, Mendes had his shirt torn, was bitten, and struck with objects, while the attacker demanded a knife to 'kill him' and threw a chair at him. Mendes stated he will pursue legal action and reaffirmed his commitment to truth despite the violence.

item.title: PF dá 15 dias para Eduardo Bolsonaro se explicar sobre abandono de cargo
item.description: The Federal Police has given former deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro 15 days to defend himself in a disciplinary proceeding for abandonment of duty, linked to his prolonged absence from Brazil since early 2025, during which he lobbied in the U.S. for his father’s acquittal. He had previously been suspended from his position as a police clerk for unjustified absences in February and lost his congressional mandate in December.

item.title: Lula recebe direitista Rodrigo Paz, presidente da Bolívia, no Planalto
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva met with Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz, a right-wing leader, at the Planalto Palace to discuss cooperation in energy, regional integration, transnational crime, trade, investment, and migration. The meeting follows Lula’s cancellation of a planned visit to Chile for the inauguration of José Antonio Kast, also a right-wing figure. Bolivia is Brazil’s main natural gas supplier, with bilateral trade reaching $2.6 billion in 2025.

item.title: PT do Rio Grande do Sul racha em discussão sobre candidatura única ou apoio ao PDT
item.description: The Workers' Party (PT) in Rio Grande do Sul is split between supporting Juliana Brizola (PDT) or running Edegar Pretto (former Conab president) as its own candidate. PDT leader Carlos Lupi sought Lula’s backing for Brizola in exchange for alliances in other states, but Lula has not yet decided, stating the outcome depends on an internal agreement in Rio Grande do Sul. Although polls show Brizola competitive against Luciano Zucco (PL) in a potential runoff, the PT faction advocating for an independent candidacy remains active.

item.title: Depois de Lula, o destino traçado para o PT é o dilúvio
item.description: The article argues that the PT will face electoral and organizational decline after Lula exits the political scene, whether in 2026 or 2030, due to its aging base, loss of connection with youth, and regionalization of support concentrated in the Northeast. Despite maintaining significant congressional benches, the party is unlikely to recover its historical size, given its structural reliance on Lula as a vote-getter. The party's failure to win in São Paulo, its birthplace, and rising disapproval among young voters reinforce the thesis of a destiny of marginalization or 'ghettoization' for the PT.

item.title: Eduardo Bolsonaro tem 15 dias para se defender em processo da PF por abandono de cargo
item.description: The Federal Police formally cited Eduardo Bolsonaro for alleged abandonment of his position as a police clerk in Angra dos Reis, giving him 15 days to present a defense. He has resided in the United States since early last year and failed to report for duty after losing his congressional seat in December 2023. The administrative proceeding may lead to his removal from office.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula recebe presidente da Bolívia
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva receives Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz at the Planalto Palace to discuss cooperation in energy, transnational crime, trade and investment. Paz, elected in October 2025, ended nearly two decades of MAS rule and assumed office amid an economic crisis marked by fuel shortages, high inflation, and declining natural gas exports.

item.title: Lula recebe Rodrigo Paz e tenta conter pressões externas nas eleições
item.description: President Lula received Bolivian leader Rodrigo Paz in an official visit to the Palácio do Planalto to strengthen ties with neighboring countries amid perceived external pressures from Washington aimed at isolating Brazil diplomatically. The meeting is part of a broader strategy to build a preventive 'ring' in the region, following the Trump-led Miami summit that excluded Brazil. The visit also provides political support to Paz ahead of Bolivia’s municipal elections on March 22 and covers cooperation on energy, infrastructure, border communities, and transnational crime.

item.title: Ministro Paulo Teixeira deixará Desenvolvimento Agrário para disputar Câmara em 2026
item.description: Minister of Agrarian Development and Family Agriculture Paulo Teixeira (PT) announced he will leave his post to run for federal deputy from São Paulo in the 2026 elections. He will remain in office until early April to comply with Brazil's six-month pre-election resignation requirement. This move is part of a broader trend, with over 20 ministers expected to step down to strengthen the government's allied base in Congress.

item.title: Zanin rejeita habeas corpus para todos os condenados pelo 8 de Janeiro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin, president of the First Chamber of the STF, rejected a habeas corpus petition filed by a right-wing lawyer on behalf of all those convicted in the January 8, 2023, coup attempts, stating that habeas corpus does not apply against acts of a minister or collegiate body of the Court. The rapporteur of the criminal cases is Alexandre de Moraes, who has criminally charged 1,399 participants in the attacks on the Three Powers, including former President Jair Bolsonaro. Most defendants were charged with minor offenses, receiving sentences of up to one year or benefiting from non-prosecution agreements.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Maré positiva para Flávio Bolsonaro faz aliados adotarem cautela
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro is adopting a cautious strategy in his pre-campaign, avoiding early presentation of government proposals to prevent creating a 'glass ceiling' that could increase his rejection. Unlike his father, he consults allies before each political move, prioritizing the formation of his ticket and defining electoral slates in states. The decision reflects an assessment that, despite strong poll numbers, releasing a liberal economic program now may not gain votes and could instead provoke backlash.

item.title: Notícias - Partido Novo
item.description: Romeu Zema, presidential pre-candidate and governor of Minas Gerais from the Novo Party, filed an impeachment request against Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes during a press conference. The move is based on alleged messages between Moraes and banker Daniel Vorcaro, which the party claims reveal abuses in the Master Case. The event was attended by dozens of Novo Party leaders nationwide.

item.title: França dos extremos: ultradireita marca avanços em eleições locais e esquerda domina Paris
item.description: In the first round of French municipal elections, the far-right made significant gains in southern cities such as Nice, Toulon, and Perpignan, while the left retained control of Paris. The results highlight deepening political polarization, forcing traditional parties to consider alliances with radical factions to win runoff elections — at the risk of damaging their ideological credibility. Leaders like Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella framed the outcome as an early sign of impending change, despite Le Pen’s current ineligibility.

item.title: Intimidação bolsonarista a jornalistas na porta de hospital vira caso de polícia
item.description: A bolsonarist militant posted a video falsely implying journalists wished for Jair Bolsonaro’s death during his hospitalization at DF Star Hospital. Former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro shared the video with her 8.1 million followers, triggering a wave of online and street harassment against reporters. Three journalists filed police reports, and unions including Fenaj and the DF Journalists’ Union appealed to authorities to prevent this incident from setting a dangerous precedent for press intimidation in the 2026 election year.

item.title: Está liberado criticar ‘O agente secreto’ sem ser crime de lesa-pátria?
item.description: The article discusses how the film 'O agente secreto', directed by Kleber Mendonça Filho, has become a target of political polarization in Brazil, praised by Lula supporters and criticized by opponents due to the director's and actor Wagner Moura's PT affiliations. It notes that the Lula government attempted to capitalize on the film's potential Oscar success, but its loss was lamented by First Lady Janja. The text also challenges the film's historical accuracy, arguing it presents a manichaeistic and incorrect portrayal of the military regime, particularly depicting Ernesto Geisel as anti-developmentalist when he actually promoted major national integration projects.

item.title: El miedo a un Gobierno con los ultras afianza el voto progresista de las personas LGTBI+
item.description: Fear of a government led by ultraconservative parties like Vox mobilizes the LGBTQ+ electorate in Spain, with 77% of those intending to vote believing their rights would be threatened. If only LGBTQ+ people voted, progressive forces (PSOE, Sumar, and Podemos) would secure an absolute majority of 202 seats in Parliament. This perception of risk is directly linked to increased electoral participation among the community.

item.title: O bolsonarista sem guerra cultural que desafia Nikolas Ferreira
item.description: Cleitinho Azevedo, a Republican senator from Minas Gerais and former pagode singer, leads gubernatorial polls with 34% voter intention, surpassing rivals like Rodrigo Pacheco (19%) and Alexandre Kalil (11%). His political career began after being boycotted from local performances for criticizing mayors, prompting him to enter politics with an anti-corruption message. He has built a family-based political network, with two brothers also elected, and positions himself as a Bolsonaro-aligned candidate who avoids culture wars, leveraging online outrage and opponent disorganization.

item.title: Joan Laporta é reeleito presidente do Barcelona até 2031
item.description: Joan Laporta was re-elected president of Barcelona with 68,18% of the votes, defeating Victor Font and securing his mandate until 2031. The election saw participation from 42% of the club’s members (48,480 votes), and Laporta emphasized that the focus will be completing the partial reconstruction of Camp Nou by 2027 and defending the club against external threats.

item.title: Pesquisa Realtime/Bigdata: Flávio Bolsonaro tem 38% e Lula tem 33% dos votos no Espírito Santo
item.description: A real-time/big data poll conducted in Espírito Santo shows Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) leading with 38% of voter intentions for the October 2026 presidential election, while President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) trails at 33%. Bolsonaro’s 5-point advantage suggests a competitive race in the state, potentially signaling a shift in the national electoral landscape.

item.title: Os principais momentos do Oscar 2026
item.description: The 2026 Oscars were dominated by 'Uma Batalha Após a Outra', which won six awards including Best Picture and Best Director. The Brazilian film 'O Agente Secreto', nominated in four categories, won none, though its star Wagner Moura served as a presenter and praised the casting team. Other notable winners were 'Pecadores' with four awards and Guillermo del Toro's 'Frankenstein' with three technical wins.

item.title: A cartada de Caiado para tentar convencer Kassab a escolhê-lo para disputa com Lula e Flávio
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, now in the PSD, is trying to stand out among the party's presidential pre-candidates, particularly by appealing to the right, such as advocating broad amnesty for those involved in the January 8 acts, to differentiate himself from more moderate rivals like Ratinho Júnior and Eduardo Leite. His goal is to convince Gilberto Kassab, PSD national president, that he is the most competitive name against Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro. Kassab is expected to decide the party's presidential candidate by the end of March.

item.title: Como saber se estão te enganando na internet — e evitar expor os amigos a fake news
item.description: The article warns about the rise of online misinformation and scams, particularly driven by deepfakes during election years, and identifies low digital literacy in Brazil as a key factor enabling the spread of fake news. It highlights the Agência Lupa’s digital investigation course, which teaches users to detect false content by checking emotional headlines, visual identity of news sources, URLs, and authors, aiming to build 'armies of fact-checkers' to combat misinformation.

item.title: Orleans Brandão adapta jingle de Lula em ato de pré-candidatura no MA
item.description: Orleans Brandão, nephew of Governor Carlos Brandão, launched his pre-candidacy for governor of Maranhão by adapting Lula's political jingle at an event with around 40,000 people and support from 182 mayors and 11 parties. Despite the reference to Lula and declared support from allies like Isnaldo Bulhões Jr., the PT is not listed among the event's party banners. The political landscape in the state is defined by the rift between Carlos Brandão and Flávio Dino, with Brandão seeking to continue his administration's policies through Orleans' candidacy.

item.title: Medida provisória reduz preço do diesel para enfrentar alta do petróleo
item.description: The federal government published Provisional Measure 1340/26 to reduce diesel prices by R$ 0,64 per liter through tax cuts and subsidies for production and import, in response to rising oil prices due to the war in Iran. The measure includes zeroing PIS and Cofins taxes on diesel and imposing new export levies of 12% on oil and 50% on diesel to offset revenue losses. It also strengthens penalties for fuel price abuses and remains in effect until December 31, 2026.

item.title: Enorme orgulho, diz Lula sobre indicações do Brasil ao Oscar
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva expressed immense pride in Brazil's Oscar 2026 nominations, congratulating Kleber Mendonça Filho, Wagner Moura, Adolpho Veloso, and the teams behind 'O Agente Secreto' and 'Sonhos de Trem', despite winning no awards. He emphasized that the five nominations reaffirm the strength of Brazilian cinema and its power to showcase national culture globally.

item.title: Estado mínimo, Bolsa Família e o impasse com a Faria Lima
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-campaign aims to reconcile a minimal state with the preservation of Bolsa Família, proposing extensive privatizations (up to 95% of state-owned enterprises), spending cuts, and tax reductions to attract investors without raising taxes. This effort to combine fiscal austerity with social protection creates discomfort in financial markets (Faria Lima), which perceive incoherence in the economic plan. The team operates discreetly but maintains intense dialogue with investors in São Paulo and Rio de Janeiro, reiterating the commitment to spend less than it collects.

item.title: Laporta é reeleito presidente do Barcelona com 68,18% dos votos
item.description: Joan Laporta was re-elected president of Barcelona with 68.18% of the vote (32,934 out of 48,480), securing his third term until 2031. He defeated Víctor Font in an election held the same day Barcelona beat Sevilla 5-2, with Raphinha scoring three goals. His new mandate will focus on maintaining domestic success amid ongoing internal tensions, particularly with figures like Xavi Hernández, who criticized Laporta’s role in Messi’s absence.

item.title: Lulistas lamentam derrota de ‘O agente secreto’: “CPI do Oscar já”
item.description: Lulist politicians lamented on social media the defeat of the Brazilian film 'O agente secreto' by Kleber Mendonça Filho at the 2026 Oscars, despite four nominations, including Best Actor for Wagner Moura. President Lula (PT) posted a message expressing pride in the film, even though it won no awards at the Sunday, March 15 ceremony.

item.title: A aposta do Centrão sobre o impacto de Vorcaro no calendário pré-eleitoral do Congresso
item.description: Centrão leaders believe the STF's decision to keep Daniel Vorcaro in prison will further empty Congress's pre-election calendar, fostering widespread distrust and ruling out investigative committees on the Master case, which could disrupt the legislative agenda. They argue that releasing Vorcaro would drag them back into the investigation, while his continued imprisonment increases pressure for a potential plea deal, despite his defense denying such intentions.

item.title: Três bons motivos para Neymar ser convocado – e outros três para ele ficar de fora
item.description: The article debates Neymar's potential call-up for Brazil's friendlies against France and Croatia, listing three arguments in favor (his goal-scoring record, enduring respect, and tactical knowledge) and three against (technical decline since 2023, advanced age with reduced physical commitment, and likely refusal to accept a bench role). It underscores the tension between his glorious past and current inconsistent form, questioning whether he still adds value to the team.

item.title: Boulos é desmentido por checagem de fatos ao vivo
item.description: Minister Guilherme Boulos was fact-checked live during an interview on Natal's 98 FM radio when he compared Lula's and Bolsonaro's reactions to investigations targeting their sons. The live fact-check revealed his statement was inaccurate, raising concerns about the reliability of his political comparisons.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro lidera contra Lula em mais um estado do Sudeste, diz Real Time Big Data
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro leads the presidential race against Lula in Espírito Santo with a narrow margin of 1 to 2 percentage points, according to Real Time Big Data. The senator ranges from 38% to 40% in voting intentions, while Lula stands between 33% and 34%. Lula is the most rejected candidate in the state (54%), followed by Bolsonaro (45%).

item.title: A briga entre Ferraço e Pazolini pelo governo do Espírito Santo, segundo o Real Time Big Data
item.description: A Real Time Big Data poll shows a technical tie between Vice Governor Ricardo Ferraço (MDB) and Vitória Mayor Lorenzo Pazolini (Republicanos) in the Espírito Santo gubernatorial race, with both at 35% in one scenario. Ferraço leads in other scenarios, while Senator Magno Malta (PL) is the most rejected candidate. Governor Renato Casagrande (PSB) maintains high approval (79%) and leads the Senate race.

item.title: O último ato de Haddad como ministro antes de embarcar em candidatura ao governo paulista
item.description: Fernando Haddad, the Finance Minister, will step down from his position to run for governor of São Paulo in 2026, at the request of President Lula, with a farewell event scheduled in the state capital. He will serve as a key campaign surrogate for Lula in the state, facing the frontrunner in polls, Tarcísio de Freitas. The ticket remains undefined, but Simone Tebet has been confirmed as his running mate, while Márcio França and Marina Silva are being considered for vice-governor and Senate, respectively.

item.title: Caiu! Tite é demitido e não é mais técnico do Cruzeiro
item.description: Coach Tite was dismissed by Cruzeiro following a draw with Vasco in the 2026 Brazilian Championship, despite the team's recent Minas Gerais state title. The decision by the board, led by Pedro Lourenço, marks a breaking point in the club's season.

item.title: Maurício, do Palmeiras, é convocado para seleção paraguaia
item.description: Palmeiras midfielder Maurício has been called up to the Paraguay national team and will play in friendlies against Greece and Morocco in March. The player, of Paraguayan descent, naturalized in February 2026 and expressed pride in representing La Albirroja.

item.title: Valdemar revela quem Tarcísio quer de vice em SP
item.description: The national president of the PL, Valdemar Costa Neto, denied having asked Eduardo Bolsonaro for help in selecting Tarcísio de Freitas’ running mate for the 2026 elections. Valdemar stated that Tarcísio intends to keep his current vice-governor, Felício Ramuth (PSD), despite PL’s efforts to promote André do Prado, president of the São Paulo Legislative Assembly, as the vice-presidential candidate. He confirmed André accompanied him to the U.S. meeting with Eduardo Bolsonaro but said the vice topic was not discussed.

item.title: França: eleições têm recorde de abstenção e avanço de partidos extremistas
item.description: France's municipal elections recorded the lowest turnout since 1958, with abstention exceeding 40%, signaling growing political polarization. Far-right (National Rally) and far-left (France Unbowed) parties made significant gains, while traditional governing parties were marginalized. The low voter turnout is seen as a warning for the upcoming runoff and the 2025 presidential election.

item.title: Três Graças: confira resumo da semana da novela das nove
item.description: In episodes 127 to 130 of the novel 'Três Graças', the conflict over the valuable sculpture intensifies with revelations of theft, betrayals, and threats of violence. Arminda and Ferente plot to kill Paulinho and Gerluce, while Kasper surprises everyone by revealing the sculpture at his exhibition, exposing Bagdá and Manoel's involvement. Tensions rise with accusations, arrests, and romantic conflicts, especially between Paulinho and Gerluce, whose secret about the sculpture threatens their relationship.
